'''Mikey Lewis''' (born 4 July 2001) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}}, {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|fb}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

==Club career==
[[File:Mikey Lewis.jpg|thumb|right|Lewis warming up for [[Hull KR]] in 2024]]

===2019===
Lewis spent time on loan at [[Newcastle Thunder]] in 2019, making seven appearances in total, scoring seven tries and adding 15 goals.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Mikey Lewis extends Newcastle stay |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mikey-lewis-extends-newcastle-stay |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=Love Rugby League |date=20 June 2019}}</ref>

After returning from Newcastle, Lewis made his début for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the 2019 season against the [[Wigan Warriors]] in a 36-18 round 25 defeat at the [[DW Stadium]] on 9 August, replacing [[Danny McGuire]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=O'Brien |first1=James |title=Mikey Lewis handed Hull KR debut at Wigan Warriors as Danny McGuire takes a rest |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/mikey-lewis-hull-kr-debut-3195631 |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=9 August 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
In 2020, Lewis made eight appearances in total for Hull KR, scoring two tries and adding five goals.

===2021===
On 12 May, Lewis signed to play for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=York bring in Hull KR youngster Mikey Lewis on loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-bring-in-hull-kr-youngster-mikey-lewis-on-loan |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=Love Rugby League |date=12 May 2021}}</ref> This was followed by a two-week loan in July<ref>{{cite news |title=Hull KR youngster Mikey Lewis rejoins York City Knights but Wembley appearance in doubt |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/mikey-lewis-hull-kr-york-5655942 |access-date=20 May 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=15 July 2021}}</ref> during which Lewis played in York's 41-34 defeat by [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[2021 RFL 1895 Cup#Final|2021 1895 Cup final]] on 17 July at [[Wembley Stadium]], scoring a single try in the fixture.<ref>{{cite news |title=1895 Cup Final: Featherstone Rovers beat York City Knights 41-34 to win at Wembley |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57873755 |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=BBC Sport |date=17 July 2021}}</ref>

Lewis made a total of 12 appearances for Hull KR during the [[2021 Super League season]], including in the club's 28-10 Super League play-off semi-final loss to the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|last1=Bower|first1=Aaron|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|work=The Guardian|location=London|date=30 September 2021 |access-date=1 October 2021}}</ref> Before the start of the semi-final, [[Sky Sports]] pundit [[Phil Clarke]] compared Lewis' 2021 season to that of [[Benji Marshall]]'s 2005 season with the [[Wests Tigers]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/phil-clarke-compares-super-league-halfback-to-benji-marshall-43757/|title=Phil Clarke compares Super League halfback to Benji Marshall |work=Serious About Rugby League |date=1 October 2021 |access-date=1 October 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 18 of the [[2022 Super League season]], Lewis scored a [[hat-trick]] for Hull KR in their 34-28 defeat by arch-rivals [[Hull F.C.]] at the [[Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/jul/10/hull-kr-hull-fc-magic-weekend-super-league-match-report/ |title=Magic Weekend: Late video referee controversy hands Hull FC derby win |work=The Guardian |location=London |date=10 July 2022 |access-date=12 July 2022}}</ref>

===2023===
In round 9 of the [[2023 Super League season]], Lewis scored two tries for Hull KR in a 26-14 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] on 14 April 2023, the club's first win over the Saints since 2015.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Appleyard |first1=Joe |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-saints-player-ratings-8351579/|title=Hull KR player ratings as Mikey Lewis and Sam Luckley shine in famous victory |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=14 April 2023 |access-date=17 April 2023}}</ref>

On 23 July, Lewis played in Hull KR's 11-10 [[2023 Challenge Cup]] semi-final victory against [[Wigan Warriors]], won in [[golden point]] extra time rules by a {{convert|40|m|abbr=on}} drop-goal from [[Brad Schneider (rugby league)|Brad Schneider]], at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]]; Lewis on the receiving end of a shoulder barge from Wigan player [[Joe Shorrocks]] within a minute of the second half restart, resulting in Shorrocks' sending-off.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12926284/challenge-cup-hull-kr-beat-wigan-in-semi-final-with-brad-schneider-kicking-golden-point-drop-goal/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in semi-final with Brad Schneider kicking golden point field goal|work=Sky Sports|date=23 July 2023|access-date=8 October 2023}}</ref> On 12 August, Lewis played for Hull KR in their 16-17 [[golden point]] extra time loss to the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[2023 Challenge Cup final]] at Wembley Stadium.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Newsum|first1=Matt|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport|date=12 August 2023|access-date=14 August 2023}}</ref> Lewis scored two tries for Hull KR in a 52-10 Super League victory over Leigh in a Challenge Cup final rematch two weeks later.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66592489|title=Super League: Hull KR 52-10 Leigh Leopards - Robins avenge Challenge Cup defeat |work=BBC Sport |date=25 August 2023 |access-date=8 October 2023}}</ref>

Lewis played in Hull KR's 20-6 Super League play-off eliminator victory against Leigh on 29 September.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Newsum |first1=Matt |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66913472 |title=Super League eliminator: Hull KR 20-6 Leigh Leopards - Hosts avenge Wembley cup loss to reach semis |work=BBC Sport |date=29 September 2023 |access-date=8 October 2023}}</ref> and on 7 October, played in Hull KR's 42-12 play-off semi-final defeat to Wigan at the [[DW Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Appleyard |first1=Joe |title=Hull KR's season comes to brutal end as Wigan Warriors thrash Robins in semi-final |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-wigan-warriors-report-8809728 |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=7 October 2023}}</ref>

===2024===
Lewis was named the Rugby League Writers and Broadcasters' Association player of the year after a [[2024 Super League season]] in which he scored 19 tries as Hull KR finished second in the Super League table.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ced0vlj6v5do|title=Lewis wins RL writers' player award|date=29 September 2024 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=30 September 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Walker|first1=Callum|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-kr-star-mikey-lewis-given-major-accolade/|title=Hull KR star Mikey Lewis given major accolade|work=Total Rugby League|access-date=30 September 2024}}</ref> He was also named the 2024 [[Man of Steel Awards|Steve Prescott MBE Man of Steel]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqlvgpeq765o |title=Hull KR's Lewis named 2024 Man of Steel |work=BBC Sport |date=8 October 2024 |access-date=9 October 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13230547/super-league-mikey-lewis-named-man-of-steel-and-georgie-hetherington-woman-of-steel-at-2024-season-awards|title=Super League: Mikey Lewis named Man of Steel and Georgie Hetherington Woman of Steel at 2024 season awards|work=Sky Sports |access-date=9 October 2024}}</ref> On 12 October, Lewis played in Hull KR's [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] 9-2 defeat by Wigan, scoring a penalty kick that would turn out to be Hull KR's only points of the fixture.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Wigan secure back-to-back titles after Bevan French's stunner sinks Hull KR |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/oct/12/wigan-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=The Guardian |date=12 October 2024 |location=London}}</ref>

===2025===
[[File:Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final and Treble Victory Parade Lewis and Trophy (cropped).jpg|thumb|right|Lewis holding the Super League trophy in 2025]]

On 13 December 2024, Hull KR announced that, from the 2025 season onwards, Lewis would wear the no. 6 shirt, which had originally been [[Retired number|retired]] in 2016 following the death of [[Roger Millward]].<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4gl2l80z0wo |title=Hull KR un-retire number six shirt for Lewis |work=BBC Sport |date=13 December 2024 |access-date=14 December 2025}}</ref> In round 1 of the [[2025 Super League season]], Lewis kicked the winning drop goal in golden point extra-time as Hull KR defeated the [[Castleford Tigers]] 19-18.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Wilkinson |first1=Jack |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/13309331/hull-kr-19-18-castleford-tigers-mikey-lewis-delivers-golden-point-magic/|title=Hull KR 19-18 Castleford Tigers: Mikey Lewis delivers golden-point magic while Hull FC comprehensively beat Catalans Dragons |work=Sky Sports |date=15 February 2025 |access-date=19 February 2025}}</ref>

Lewis was a central figure in the squad that brought Hull KR its historic first [[treble (rugby league)|treble]] during 2025. On 7 June, Lewis played in Hull KR's 8-6 [[2025 Challenge Cup final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves]], taking over goal-kicking duties from [[cup-tied]] [[Arthur Mourgue]]. Lewis converted a penalty in the first half, and scored the match-winning conversion following a late try by [[Tom Davies (rugby league)|Tom Davies]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Wellens |first1=Megan |title=Hull KR Challenge Cup hero Mikey Lewis shows star power to help Rovers end 40 years of hurt |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13380515/hull-kr-challenge-cup-hero-mikey-lewis-shows-star-power-to-help-rovers-end-40-years-of-hurt |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=Sky Sports |date=7 June 2025}}</ref> On 18 September, Lewis played in Hull KR's 28-20 final round victory over Warrington in the fixture that ensured they lifted the [[League Leaders Shield]],<ref>{{cite news |last1=Brennan |first1=Stuart |title=Hull KR see off Wire to win League Leaders' Shield |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo |access-date=6 November 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=18 September 2025}}</ref> and on 9 October, Lewis played in Hull KR's [[2025 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan, scoring the first try of the fixture and winning the [[Rob Burrow Award]] after being voted [[Player of the Match]].<ref>{{cite news|last1=Freeman|first1=Jay|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|work=BBC Sport |date=11 October 2025 |access-date=13 October 2025}}</ref>

===2026===
In round 1 of the [[2026 Super League season]], Lewis was given a yellow card for a deliberate trip on an opposition player during Hull Kingston Rovers shock loss to the newly promoted [[York Knights]]. Lewis later avoided a ban for the trip, clearing him to play in the [[2026 World Club Challenge]] against [[Brisbane Broncos]] on 19 February.<ref>{{cite news|last1=McAllister|first1=Josh|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/mikey-lewis-disciplinary-verdict-explained-10817785|title=Mikey Lewis disciplinary verdict explained with Hull KR star's World Club Challenge fate set|work=Hull Daily Mail|date=16 February 2026|access-date=19 February 2026}}</ref> Lewis played as {{rlp|fb}} in the Hull KR squad that won against Brisbane 30-24 at cross-city rivals [[Hull F.C.]]'s [[MKM Stadium]] and awarded Hull KR the title of rugby league world champions, admitting in a post-match interview that the trip was "a stupid thing I did" and that he "wanted to earn [the squad's] trust back" over the course of the fixture.<ref>{{cite news |title=World Club Challenge: Mikey Lewis redeems himself on night Hull KR conquer the world |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12217/13509789/world-club-challenge-mikey-lewis-redeems-himself-on-night-hull-kr-conquer-the-world |access-date=19 March 2026 |work=Sky Sports |date=19 February 2026}}</ref>
In round 9 of the Super League season, Lewis scored four tries for Hull Kingston Rovers as they defeated Bradford 48-12.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/mikey-lewis-runs-riot-hull-10935835/|title=Mikey Lewis runs riot as Hull KR cruise to victory over Bradford Bulls|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
[[File:Mikey Lewis kicking off for England.jpg|thumb|Lewis kicking off for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Wembley Stadium]] in the first test of the [[2025 Kangaroo tour of England|2025 Ashes Series]]]]

Lewis made his senior [[England national rugby league team|England]] début against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the first test of the [[2023 Tonga rugby league tour of England|2023 series]] on 22 October 2023, scoring a try and winning Player of the Match.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Smith |first1=Emma |title=England 22-18 Tonga: Mikey Lewis 'loved every second' of emotional international debut |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67189152 |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=BBC Sport |date=22 October 2023}}</ref>

Lewis was called up to England for the [[2025 Kangaroo tour of England|2025 Ashes Series]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], playing as a {{rlp|hb}} in the first test at Wembley Stadium on 25 October 2025;<ref>{{cite news |last1=Whalley |first1=Mike |title=Lewis in England squad for first Test as Smith left out |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cge5n125z8eo |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=BBC Sport |date=23 October 2025}}</ref> Lewis scored a conversion kick following England's only try, scored by [[Daryl Clark]], in their 6-26 defeat by Australia.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Smith |first1=Emma |title=Australia crush England in first Ashes match for 22 years |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ceq0r4vg1j1o |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=BBC Sport |date=25 October 2025}}</ref> Lewis was a {{rlp|in}} in the second test at the [[Hill Dickinson Stadium]] on 2 November,<ref>{{cite news |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=England team confirmed for second Ashes Test as Mikey Lewis role confirmed |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/england-team-second-ashes-test-1479497 |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=All Out Rugby League |date=1 November 2025}}</ref> taking on the same position in the third and final test at Headingley on 8 November.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Smith |first1=Emma |title=Australia see off England for Ashes clean sweep |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czr1ek144jlo |access-date=7 February 2026 |work=BBC Sport |date=8 November 2025}}</ref>
